name: Webb's Fabulous Pharmacies, Inc. v. Beckwith
summary: The Court held that a state may not use its own definition of property to avoid the requirements of the Fifth Amendment's Takings Clause, rejecting the idea that the government can simply legislate away a protected property interest.
significance: Stands for the principle that a state cannot define what constitutes 'property' in a way that effectively reads the Takings Clause out of the Constitution—the anti-circularity rule central to petitioner's argument.
